claims.claim: High oxalate content hinders calcium absorption
claims.mechanism: Oxalates chelate calcium forming insoluble calcium oxalate that cannot be absorbed in the intestine

consumption.amount: Can eat for other nutrients but not as calcium source
consumption.reasoning: Still nutritious but unreliable calcium sources due to oxalate content

relationships.description: Complement with low-oxalate vegetables for calcium needs

claims.claim: Consumption associated with lower risk of developing cancer and cardiovascular disease
claims.mechanism: Second highest protein content of legumes (27%). One of highest concentrations of phenolic compounds and antioxidants among legumes.

consumption.preparation: Cook 15-20 min (red) or 10 min in pressure cooker. Sprout for salads. Use in soups, dahl, pasta sauces, masala dosa.
consumption.reasoning: 1 cup boiled green lentils: 230 cal, 16g fibre, 82% RDA iron, 90% RDA folate

claims.claim: Regular consumption helps prevent accumulation of belly fat and chronic disease
claims.mechanism: Fibre-rich food that after gut microbe processing releases protective metabolites; low in empty calories compared to processed foods

claims.claim: Part of fibre-rich diet that causes spontaneous weight loss and metabolic improvement
claims.mechanism: High fibre induces early satiety; source of plant protein, vitamins, phytocompounds and minerals

consumption.reasoning: Key component of the Mediterranean-style study diet

relationships.description: Part of the core fibre-rich food group

claims.claim: Consuming only non-starchy vegetables on fasting days powerfully shuts down the insulin/IGF-1/mTOR pro-ageing pathway
claims.mechanism: Absence of animal protein, grains and fruit combined with very low calorie content (~500 cal/day) inhibits the insulin/IGF-1/mTOR pathway that modulates ageing and cancer development
claims.study_ref: Refs 46, 47 in book; author's own clinical studies

claims.claim: Vegetable fasting 2-3 times per week causes significant weight loss without calorie counting
claims.mechanism: Very low calorie density of non-starchy vegetables keeps intake below 500 cal/day, achieving ~20-23% weekly calorie restriction

claims.claim: Vegetable fasting activates longevity pathways via hormesis
claims.mechanism: Fasting acts as chronic mild stressor (hormetic agent) that activates cellular protective and anti-ageing pathways, increases stress resistance, elevates cortisol moderately, and enhances removal of damaged proteins

claims.claim: Fasting increases brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF)
claims.mechanism: BDNF promotes growth and survival of nerve cells, strengthening brain function

consumption.amount: Unrestricted non-starchy vegetables, raw or cooked, keeping total daily intake under ~500 calories
consumption.frequency: 2-3 times per week (depending on baseline weight and metabolic profile)
consumption.preparation: Raw or cooked, dressed with 1 tablespoon extra-virgin olive oil, lemon juice or vinegar at lunch and dinner. No more than 2 tablespoons oil total per fasting day
consumption.reasoning: Author's clinical protocol for intermittent vegetable fasting; superior to 5:2 diet because excluding animal protein, grains and fruit maximises mTOR pathway suppression

relationships.description: Olive oil used as dressing on fasting days; each tablespoon adds ~100 calories so limited to 2 per day
Used as dressing alongside olive oil on fasting day vegetables

claims.claim: Plain dark chocolate (rich in epicatechin) increases plasma antioxidant capacity. Effect markedly reduced when consumed with milk.
claims.mechanism: Epicatechin is a potent antioxidant flavonoid. Milk proteins bind to epicatechin preventing absorption.

consumption.preparation: Plain dark chocolate only, NOT milk chocolate. Consume without dairy.
consumption.reasoning: Milk blocks the antioxidant absorption (Nature study)

relationships.description: Author combines dark cocoa powder with brazil nuts in morning smoothies

claims.claim: Consuming vegetables at each meal prevents chronic disease and maximises health
claims.mechanism: Provides unique mixture of vegetable fibres, vitamins, oligo-elements and phytochemicals which after gut microbe processing release metabolites that protect from common diseases

consumption.reasoning: Historical recommendation from Sun Simiao (Tang dynasty) supported by modern evidence on fibre-rich foods and gut microbiome metabolites

relationships.description: Part of the recommended fibre-rich food group alongside beans, whole grains, nuts, seeds and fruit

name: Green Vegetables (lettuce, cabbage, broccoli, spinach, arugula, chard, kale)

claims.claim: Almost undetectable effect on blood glucose
claims.mechanism: Very low carbohydrate content and high fibre means virtually no glucose load

consumption.amount: Abundant; 1 cup cabbage + 1 cup spinach in sample diet
consumption.reasoning: Essentially zero glycaemic impact while providing vitamins, minerals and fibre

claims.claim: 3-4 cups per day achieves largest risk reduction for cardiovascular and cancer outcomes
claims.mechanism: Not fully specified in this chapter; epidemiological association

claims.claim: Relatively high concentration of vegetable omega-3 fatty acids
claims.mechanism: 50g fresh purslane contains approximately 200mg alpha-linolenic acid

consumption.amount: Add to salads from time to time
consumption.preparation: Raw stems and leaves in salads. Slightly sour and salty taste.
consumption.reasoning: Wild edible weed with unusually high omega-3 for a plant

claims.claim: Consuming during meals reduces absorption of iron, zinc and other minerals
claims.mechanism: Polyphenols (tannins) in tea, coffee and cocoa chelate iron and other minerals, reducing their bioavailability

consumption.frequency: Consume between meals, not with food
consumption.reasoning: Reduces mineral absorption when consumed alongside iron/zinc-containing plant foods

relationships.description: Opposes the mineral absorption-enhancing effect of vitamin C

claims.claim: One of the richest sources of dietary flavonoids important for cardiovascular disease and cancer prevention, especially gastric cancer
claims.mechanism: Contains fructooligosaccharides and thiosulfinates. Flavonoids prevent cardiovascular disease and cancer.

claims.claim: Italian sweet red onion Tropea contains taxifolin, a powerful inhibitor of many cancer cell types
claims.mechanism: Taxifolin directly inhibits cultured cancer cell growth. Red onions have highest flavonol levels; yellow onions contain half as much.

consumption.preparation: Red onions preferred for highest flavonol content. Raw or cooked.
consumption.reasoning: Red varieties contain double the flavonols of yellow onions

claims.claim: Contain the highest concentration of beta-carotene among common vegetables
claims.mechanism: 31mg beta-carotene per 100g cooked, converted to vitamin A for eye health and immune function

consumption.preparation: Cooked, paired with fat for absorption
consumption.reasoning: Highest beta-carotene concentration of common vegetables

claims.claim: Superior source of antioxidants due to generous amounts of alpha-carotene, zeaxanthin and lutein
claims.mechanism: Multiple carotenoid types provide broader antioxidant coverage than beta-carotene alone

consumption.preparation: Can eat thin slices raw, roast, bake, use in soups, or puree with chickpeas for hummus. Add to morning smoothies.
consumption.reasoning: Versatile, sweet flavour makes it easy to incorporate

claims.claim: Rich in lycopene and vitamin C providing antioxidant protection
claims.mechanism: Lycopene is a potent carotenoid antioxidant; vitamin C neutralises free radicals

claims.claim: Contain lycopene important in prevention of prostate, digestive tract and cervical cancers
claims.mechanism: Lycopene reduces IGF-I bioavailability, which may mediate anti-cancer effect. RCT showed reduction in prostate cancer growth with lycopene supplementation before surgery.

consumption.preparation: Fresh diced, as sauce, sun-dried preserved in EVOO; paired with garlic, basil, oregano
consumption.reasoning: Ubiquitous in traditional Mediterranean cuisine as described throughout chapter

relationships.description: Fat from EVOO enhances absorption of fat-soluble lycopene from tomatoes
Traditional pairing providing complementary phytochemical profiles

claims.claim: Far lower calorie density than cheese while providing fibre and protein
claims.mechanism: 100g cooked chickpeas = 164 cal (2.6g fat, 27g carbs incl 7g fibre, 9g protein) vs 100g Parmesan = 384 cal (28g fat, 0g fibre, 33g protein); fibre induces early satiety

claims.claim: Concentrated source of protein, fibre, vitamins, minerals and low-glycaemic starches
claims.mechanism: Complex carbohydrates with high amylose content slowly digested, low glycaemic index

consumption.preparation: Soak 24 hours, cook 1 hour. Use in salads, soups, hummus, falafel, farinata.
consumption.reasoning: 1 cup boiled: 270 cal, 12g fibre, 15g protein, 64% RDA copper, 73% RDA manganese, 40% RDA folate

relationships.description: Chickpeas presented as healthier alternative to calorie-dense, fibreless Parmesan cheese
Combined in hummus for complete amino acid profile and healthy fats

claims.claim: Moderate consumption with meals is part of the traditional Mediterranean dietary pattern associated with lower cardiovascular mortality
claims.mechanism: Polyphenols (resveratrol, anthocyanins) provide antioxidant effects; consumed only with meals to moderate absorption

consumption.preparation: Consumed only with food, never independently
consumption.reasoning: Traditional pattern: 'a glass of red wine was consumed regularly but only with meals'

relationships.description: Together accounted for ~25% of calories in traditional Neapolitan diet surveyed by Keys
